response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software developer with a strong polyglot background, but the role specifically demands a C++ developer with 8-12 years of hands-on C++, STL, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity expertise. The resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with the bulk of the career in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. No evidence of industrial automation, MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL, or relevant protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.).
strengths: Solid senior-level engineering experience across web, embedded, and distributed systems
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops on testing and architecture
Some exposure to networked, event-driven, and high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby microservices)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, over a decade ago — far from the depth required
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, SDL, industrial cybersecurity, or automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)
No industrial automation or IoT platform background; cloud experience is AWS, not Azure as preferred

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for a C++ developer role
Missing industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL expertise
Strong general senior engineering and mentoring track record
